title: minds widgets — rich MCP widgets in Codex & Claude Desktop
_comment: Goal: extend minds-ai-co/codex-plugin so minds MCP query results (panel responses, mind cards) render as rich Ahrefs-scorecard-style widgets inside Codex and the Claude Desktop GUI, instead of plain text. Final state: a minds panel query renders a rich widget in BOTH Codex and Claude Desktop (screenshot proof in each), with the render path documented (client-side plugin vs server-emitted). Mode: BUILD, but feasibility-spike-first — workstream #1 proves one widget renders in each client, then HARD-PAUSE for Sean's go/no-go before building the full set. Repo/worktree: minds-ai-co/codex-plugin at ~/Desktop/codex-plugin/.worktrees/widgets, branch 'widgets' off main. Workspace slug 'minds-widgets' (tmux session still macbook-widgets on the live team; realigns to macbook-minds-widgets on next fresh launch). System of record: GitHub issues in minds-ai-co/codex-plugin. Team (LEAN): orchestrator + frontend-lead (widget UI/HTML) + code-review-lead. Escalate to backend-lead + coordinate with the live mcp-layer team ONLY if feasibility shows widgets must be server-emitted from minds-ai-co/webapp's MCP endpoint. Flagged risk: Ahrefs-style scorecards are server-emitted (render_with metadata + render tools) — if minds needs that, the real work crosses into minds-ai-co/webapp (the surface mcp-layer is re-architecting), which is a pause+ask gate.
